Quick Facts
  • Samantha was born without her left hand. After several years playing soccer, she started to swim in 2013 before moving to the U.S. Olympic & Paralympic Training Center
  • Daughter of Ray Tubbs
  • Hobbies include reading, swimming, watching movies and hanging out with friends
  • Volunteers at a local YMCA teaching grade schoolers how to swim

bio.education: Sarasota Military Academy (Sarasota, Fla.) ‘19 University of Kentucky '23, Kinesiology
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Jennifer Tucker Mogensen
  • One younger brother, Reece
  • Hobbies include riding horses
  • Picked up rifle shooting in high school, training most days in her family’s garage
  • Once referred to as the “rebel child” of shooting, she quit her high school team and learned to teach herself via YouTube videos and online resources
  • Named Rookie Shooter of the Year as a freshman at University of Kentucky
  • Athlete of the Year sophomore year
  • First UK athlete to sweep gold in both events at NCAA Championships (Air and Smallbore Rifle)
  • Less than a week later, competed at ISSF World Cup New Delhi where she won gold, silver and bronze medal
  • Has a cat named Trigger
  • Doesn't have a sense of smell
Olympic Experience
  • 2-time Olympian; 1-time Olympic medalist (1 silver)
    • Olympic Games Paris 2024, 13th (10m Air Rifle - Team - Mixed), 25th (50m Rifle 3 Positions - Women), 32th (10m Air Rifle - Women)
    • Olympic Games Tokyo 2020, silver (10m Air Rifle - Team - Mixed), 6th (10m Air Rifle - Women), 13th (50m Rifle 3 Positions - Women)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2023 – 13th (10m Air Rifle - Women), 33th (50m Rifle Prone - Women), 49th (50m Rifle 3 Positions - Women)
  • Years of participation: 10m Air Rifle - Women 2022, 2023; 50m Rifle 3 Positions - Women 2023; 50m Rifle Prone - Women 2022, 2023; 10m Air Rifle - Team - Women 2022; 50m Rifle 3 Positions - Team - Mixed 2022; 50m Rifle 3 Positions - Team - Women 2022; 50m Rifle Prone - Team (Mixed) - Mixed 2022
  • Medals: 2 (1 silver, 1 bronze)
    • Silver – 2022 (10m Air Rifle - Team - Women)
    • Bronze – 2022 (50m Rifle Prone - Women)

Quick Facts
  • One of 32 siblings, all of whom share a father
  • Became a U.S. citizen in 2016
  • Hobbies include sewing and watching movies
  • Calls herself a people person
  • Has worked as an Uber driver
  • Avid crocheter having made and sold more than 1,000 beanies on Etsy.
Olympic Experience
  • One-time Olympian (2020)
  • Olympic Games Tokyo 2020, no result (marathon)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2017 – 15th (cross country)
  • Years of Participation: 2017
  • Top finish: 15th – 2017 (cross country)

bio.education: Abraham Lincoln High School (Council Bluffs, Iowa) ‘97 Southwest Minnesota State University '02, Political Science
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Raised in a basketball family
  • Son of John and Luellen Turek
  • Married to Jaralon
  • Has four siblings, Ulette Bonner, Rachell, John and Elisha
  • Shoots basketball every day for an hour and lifts four times a week
  • Continued the tradition of playing the sport at the highest level at Southwest Minnesota State University as the program’s most prolific scorer in history
  • He, his brother John, and sister Elisha, all played collegiately across the United States and professionally in Europe
  • Was first introduced to the sport through a wheelchair sports camp in Omaha, Nebraska
  • Made his first national team in 2004 before his Paralympic Games debut in Athens later that year
Paralympic Experience
  • 4-time Paralympian; 3-time Paralympic medalist (2 gold, 1 bronze)
    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, gold (Team - Men)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, gold (Team - Men)
    • Paralympic Games London 2012, bronze (Team - Men)
    • Paralympic Games Athens 2004, 7th (Team - Men)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2014 – silver (Team - Men)
  • Years of participation: Team - Men 2014
  • Medals: 1 (1 silver)
    • Silver – 2014 (Team - Men)

bio.education: Lakeridge High School Oregon State University
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Son of Loretta Mabinton and Ayanti Udo-Udoma
  • Was a high school four-sport varsity letter winner in wrestling, tennis, football and track
  • Won the 2012 Harlem Globetrotters' talent search contest
  • Was the leading try-scorer of the 2019 USA Touch Rugby Men’s Open World Cup Team
  • Was elected to the USA Team Handball Board of Directors as the Beach Athlete Representative in March 2021
  • Saved a penalty shot from Kevin Hart
Career Highlights
  • 2023 Mexican Vulcán Cup All-Star Game - 1st Place, Captain
  • 2022 USA Team Handball Men’s Beach Athlete of the Year
  • 2022 Desafio Internacional De Handebol De Praia - 2nd, Captain (Rio De Janiero, Brazil)
  • 2022 So. Cal Beach Handball Championships - 2nd, Captain
  • 2022 Polish Beach Handball Club Championships - 3rd, Best Right Wing Nomination (Plock, Poland)
  • 2022 IWGA World Games - 4th, Assists Leader (Birmingham, USA)
  • 2022 Egypt Pre-WC series - 2nd, Captain (Cairo, Egypt)
  • 2022 North American & Caribbean Handball Confederation (NACHC) Championships - 1st, Captain (Acapulco, Mexico)
  • 2022 Copa Internacional de Balonmano Playa Volcán - 2nd, Captain
  • 2021 European Handball Federation Champions Cup - 2nd (Isola delle Femmine, Italy)
  • 2021 USA vs Europe All-Star Game - 1st, Captain
  • 2021 So. Cal Beach Handball Championships - 2nd, Captain
  • 2021 Arena Handball Tour Nazaré, Portugal - Quarterfinals
  • 2021 Arena Handball Tour Orihuela, Spain
  • 2021 Torneo Internacional De España Gran Canaria - 5th (Gran Canary Islands, Spain)
  • 2021 European Beach Handball Tour - 3rd (Málaga, Spain)
  • International World Games Association - Athlete Of The Month (June 2020)
  • 2020 Copa Internacional de Balonmano Playa Volcán - 1st, Captain, MVP
  • 2019 Brazilian Beach Handball National Championships - 5th
  • 2019 ANOC World Beach Games - 11th, Assists Leader (Doha, Qatar)
  • 2019 So. Cal Beach Handball Championships - 1st, Captain
  • 2019 North American & Caribbean Handball Confederation (NORCA) Championships - 1st, MVP (Trinidad & Tobago)
  • 2019 Copa Internacional de Balonmano Playa Volcán - 3rd, MVP
  • 2018 So. Cal Beach Handball Championships - 1st, MVP
  • 2018 Chinese Beach Handball National Championships - 1st, Captain
  • 2018 Pan American Beach Handball Championships - 3rd
  • 2018 Taça Kika Invitational - Quarterfinals (João Pessoa, Brazil)
  • 2017 So. Cal Beach Handball Championships - 3rd
  • 2017: Named to the International World Games Association Most Important Beach Handball Players’ List
  • 2017 Prague Open Beach Handball Championships - 2nd
  • 2017 Australian Beach Handball National Championships - 2nd
  • 2017 Taça Kika Invitational - Quarterfinals (João Pessoa, Brazil)
  • 2017 Beach Games Seynois – 2nd (La Seyne, France)
  • 2016 So. Cal Beach Handball Championships - 2nd, MVP
  • 2016 Pan American Beach Handball Championships – 1st (Vargas, Venezuela)
  • 2015: Named to Senior Men’s National Team
  • 2015 American Open Beach Handball Championships - 2nd
World Championship Experience
  • 2022 IHF Beach Handball World Championships - 12th (Crete, Greece)
  • 2018 IHF Beach Handball World Championships - 12th (Kazan, Russia)
  • 2016 IHF Beach Handball World Championships – 11th (Budapest, Hungary)

Quick Facts
  • Wears No. 28
  • Parents are Rolando and Stacy Udria
  • Father previously played for USA Baseball Junior National Team
  • Personal motto: Everything happens for a reason
  • Three words to describe her are: confident, passionate and loyal
  • What non-softball sport would she like to try: I would love to play volleyball
  • On what it means to play for USA Softball: "It is an absolute honor to represent the United States and to be able to play the highest level of softball possible."
Career Highlights
  • 2018 USA Softball International Cup; Hit .333 (4-for-12) with one HR, six RBI and five runs scored

Quick Facts
  • Uhlaender loves to ski, snowboard, kite board and craves speed, which is one of the reasons she began skeleton.
  • Began competing in skeleton in 2004
  • She "loves the feeling of getting power off the start block."
  • Uhlaender also enjoys photography, writing, film, and her favorite book is "The Fountainhead."
  • After her sliding career, Katie would like to become a producer or writer so that she can combine her "writing and photographic skills in a creative way."

  • 2017-2018 World Cup- 9th in Lake Placid (11/17); 7th in Park City (11/17); 9th in Whistler (11/17); 8th in Winterberg (12/17); 28th in Igls (12/17); 10th in St. Moritz (1/18); 8th in Koenigssee (1/18);
  • 2017-2018 Intercontinental Cup - SILVER in St. Moritz #1 (1/18); GOLD in St. Moritz #2 (1/18);
  • 2016-2017 World Cup - 12th in Altenberg (1/17); 20th in Winterberg (1/17); 12th in St. Moritz (1/17); 8th in Koenigssee (1/17); 14th in Igls (2/17); 8th in PyeongChang (3/17)
  • 2015-2016 Intercontinental Cup - GOLD in Lake Placid #1 (11/15); GOLD in Lake Placid #2 (11/15); BRONZE in Whistler #1 (12/15); SILVER in Whistler #2 (12/15); BRONZE in Igls #1 (1/16); BRONZE in Igls #2 (1/16)
  • 2015-2016 North American Cup- GOLD in Whistler #2 (11/15)
  • 2013-2014 World Cup- 12th in Calgary (11/13), 14th in Park City (12/13), 14th in Lake Placid #1 (12/13),9th in Lake Placid #2 (12/13), DNS Winterberg (1/14), 6th in St. Moritz (1/14), 12th in Igls (1/14), 11th in Koenigssee (1/14)

  • 2011-2012 Intercontinental Cup- BRONZE in Calgary #1 (2/12), 6th in Calgary #2 (2/12)

  • 2007-2008 World Cup- 7th in Calgary (11/07), SILVER in Park City (12/07), GOLD in Lake Placid (12/07), 4th in Cesana #1 (1/08), GOLD in Cesana #2 (1/08), GOLD in St. Moritz (1/08), GOLD in Königssee (2/08), BRONZE at Winterberg (2/08)
